content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
J. Mark Atlas 
Law Offices of Frost, Krup 
and Atlas 
134 West Sycamore Street 
willows, Ca 
95988 
Dear Mr. Atlas: 
July 7, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-160 
We have received your request for advice concerning the 
Northeast willows Community Services District (the 
"district"). 
You are requesting this advice as attorney for 
the district. 
QUESTION 
May the district use public funds to reimburse one of its 
directors for accountant's fees incurred by the director for 
preparation of his statement of economic interests? 
CONCLUSION 
The Political Reform Act (the "Act")Y does not address the 
question of whether the district may use public funds to 
reimburse a director for accountant's fees he incurred for 
preparation of his statement of economic interests. 
You may 
wish to seek assistance on this issue from the county counsel's 
office or from the attorney general's office. 
FACTS 
The directors are required to prepare and file statements 
of economic interests. 
One of the directors had his statement 
prepared by his accountant, and has now presented the district 
with a bill for reimbursement of the accountant's fees. 
Y 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


J. Mark Atlas 
July 7, 1987 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
section 87302 requires that designated employees within 
state and local government agencies file periodic statements 
disclosing reportable investments, interests in real property 
and income. 
The Act does not address the issue of whether 
public funds may be used to reimburse a public official for 
fees the official pays to an accountant to prepare the 
statement. 
You may wish to seek advice from the county 
counsel's office or the attorney general's office regarding the 
propriety of using public funds for this purpose. 
If you have any questions, please contact me at 
(916) 322-5901. 
DMG:JGM:plh 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
~
1.~~ 
J 
n G. McLean 
Co nsel, Legal Division 


LAW OFFICES 
FROST, KRU P AND ATLAS 
CHARLES H. FROST 
LEONARD C. KRUP 
J. MARK ATLAS, 
LAW COR..PORATION 
TELEPHONE (916) 934-5416 
June 4, 1987 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Gentlemen: 
Re: 
Northeast 
llows Community 
Services District 
I am the attorney for the Northeast Willows Community 
Services District and I have been presented with a somewhat 
novel question. 
The Directors were of course requi 
to prepare and 
file Forms 730. 
One of the Directors had his form prepared by 
his accountant, and has now presented the District with a bill 
for reimbursement of the accountant's fees. 
The fees are not 
substantial, but there is a question in my mind whether a 
district can use public funds for this purpose. 
I 
refore would appreciate any guidance you can 
provide. 
Thank you. 
Sincerely 
~/~/' 
J. MAR 
JMA:eb
